ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

Ms. Rasch stated that this is one case where the 1% guideline fee is warranted, that this case was in huge disarray, that it required a great deal of expertise and that if a less experienced fiduciary had been appointed there would have been far, far higher Attorney fees. Ms. Rasch also stated that Ms. Dolch's experience is detailed in the declaration, that the Trust was not under Court supervision, that all of the assets of the Conservatorship were turned over to the Trust, that the Trustee was completely in over his head, that he made very risky investments/hard money loans, and that there were tenant issues and squatters. In addition, Ms. Rasch stated that there were very difficult situations, that records were poor to non-existent, that they had to get records from third parties, that they had to track down assets, that the Former Trustee sold property for below market value, that there was a lawsuit and that there are a lot of moving parts. Furthermore, Ms. Rasch stated that they are still trying to find heirs, that there was an IRS audit and that they did mediation in the litigation matter which resulted in settlement. The Amended First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
